question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R6 changes its Loopback0 prefix-SID from `index 6` to `index 4` — the
same SID already used by R4. SR control-plane prefers the LSP with the
lower system-id (or earliest install) and rejects the conflicting
prefix-SID. One router (likely R6) loses its SID-based reachability;
the other (R4) keeps it. Traffic engineering paths anchored on the
loser's loopback break.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on R6, the Loopback0 prefix-SID was changed from index 6 to index 4, colliding with R4's existing index 4; both indices resolve to the same MPLS label (16004), so two distinct loopback prefixes (192.0.2.4/32 and 192.0.2.6/32) claim one global SID.
A prefix-SID label must map to exactly one prefix, so IS-IS SR cannot install both and rejects the loser's prefix-SID (likely R6's); that loopback loses its SID-based label binding and TE paths anchored on it cannot be programmed.
Fix: restore R6's Loopback0 prefix-SID to index 6.
expected_symptoms_summary:
%ROUTING-ISIS-6-PREFIX_SID_INFO reported by R0, R1, R2 (3 instances):
- R0: ISIS (LAB): Prefix SID info: 'Label Conflict: Prefix-SID label 16004 for algo SPF is used by 192.0.2.4/32 and 192.0.2.6/32'
- R1: ISIS (LAB): Prefix SID info: 'Label Conflict: Prefix-SID label 16004 for algo SPF is used by 192.0.2.4/32 and 192.0.2.6/32'
- R2: ISIS (LAB): Prefix SID info: 'Label Conflict: Prefix-SID label 16004 for algo SPF is used by 192.0.2.4/32 and 192.0.2.6/32'

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router isis LAB
interface Loopback0
 address-family ipv4 unicast
  prefix-sid index 4

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router isis LAB
interface Loopback0
 address-family ipv4 unicast
  prefix-sid index 6
